measures.label: select * from `lessons` where `lessons`.`course_id` in (1, 2, 3, 4) order by `position` asc
select `image`, `author`, `star`, `body` from `testimonials` where (`status` = ?) order by `id` desc
select `image` from `sponsors` where (`status` = ?) order by `id` desc
select `question`, `answer` from `home_page_faqs` where (`status` = ?) order by `order` asc

hints: Use SELECT * only if you need all columns from table
LIMIT without ORDER BY causes non-deterministic results, depending on the query execution plan

statements.sql: select `image`, `name`, `id`, `thumbnail`, `total_courses` from `categories` where (`status` = '1') order by `id` desc limit 4

select `id`, `type`, `slug`, `title`, `thumbnail`, `price`, `discount_price`, `mode_of_delivery` from `courses` where (`type` = '1' and `status` = '1') and `scope` = 1 order by `total_enrolled` desc limit 4
select * from `lessons` where `lessons`.`course_id` in (1, 2, 3, 4) order by `position` asc
hints: Use SELECT * only if you need all columns from table

statements.sql: select `image`, `name`, `id`, `thumbnail`, `total_courses` from `categories` where (`status` = '1') order by `id` desc
select `id`, `type`, `slug`, `title`, `thumbnail`, `price`, `discount_price` from `courses` where (`type` = '2' and `status` = '1') and `scope` = 1 order by `id` desc limit 4
select `image`, `author`, `star`, `body` from `testimonials` where (`status` = '1') order by `id` desc
select `image` from `sponsors` where (`status` = '1') order by `id` desc
select `slug`, `thumbnail`, `authored_date`, `title` from `blogs` where (`status` = '1') order by `id` desc limit 4
select `question`, `answer` from `home_page_faqs` where (`status` = '1') order by `order` asc
